codeReview.summary: ResearcherAgent implementation is partially correct but has blocking issues: (1) SearchDepth is duplicated instead of reusing existing search_router::SearchDepth, (2) ResearchResult/ResearchFinding types not exported from lib.rs breaking discoverability, (3) web_search tools exist but are not wired into tool registry causing stub mode fallback.

issues.description: Duplicate SearchDepth enum defined in agents.rs when search_router.rs already has a more sophisticated version with configurable quick_indicators, deep_indicators, and min_deep_length. ResearcherAgent::classify_depth() is a simplified static version that doesn't reuse SearchRouter::classify_depth(). The duplicate enum causes confusion and the simpler classification logic may produce different results than the dedicated router.

ResearchResult, ResearchFinding, and SearchDepth (from agents.rs) are not exported from the orchestrator crate's lib.rs. Other agent result types (EvaluationResult, ReviewResult, RefactorResult, etc.) are exported. This breaks discoverability - consumers cannot find these types without directly inspecting agents.rs.

max_iterations is hardcoded to 5 in all builder methods (new(), with_llm(), with_tools(), with_llm_and_tools()). This magic number should be loaded from .swell/settings.json per project conventions (AGENTS.md states: 'Never hardcode magic numbers - always load from .swell/settings.json or environment variables').

Behavioral invariants added for Researcher role ('must return findings', 'must track sources') have satisfied: false by default. These are placeholder invariants that don't actually verify anything. Either implement actual verification logic or remove these invariants as they provide no value.

WebSearchTool, DomainSearchTool, and FetchPageTool are implemented in web_search.rs (1252 lines) but the module is not registered in swell-tools/src/mod.rs. The worker handoff says 'web search tools are not yet implemented' but they exist - they're just not wired up. This is a shared state/documentation gap rather than an implementation gap.

sharedStateObservations.observation: ResearchResult and ResearchFinding types are defined in agents.rs but not exported from lib.rs, violating the established pattern where all public agent result types are exported. EvaluationResult, ReviewResult, RefactorResult, etc. are all in lib.rs exports.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib.rs exports: EvaluationResult, ReviewResult, RefactorResult, etc. but not ResearchResult. grep 'ResearchResult' in lib.rs returns no matches.

sharedStateObservations.observation: max_iterations hardcoded to 5 instead of loading from settings. AGENTS.md explicitly states 'Never hardcode magic numbers - always load from .swell/settings.json'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: agents.rs lines 2193-2223: max_iterations: 5 is set in all builder methods. No settings.json lookup.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skill (rust) says to use thiserror::Error for errors, but SwellError is used directly from swell_core. This appears correct for this codebase, but the skill could be clearer about when to use SwellError vs custom error types.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: agents.rs uses SwellError from swell_core for all error cases. Skill mentions thiserror::Error but this project uses SwellError which wraps thiserror internally.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Web search tools (WebSearchTool, DomainSearchTool, FetchPageTool) exist in web_search.rs but are not registered in swell-tools/src/mod.rs. The handoff claims tools 'are not yet implemented' but they exist - just not wired. This disconnect between handoff and reality suggests either the worker didn't discover the existing tools or there's a registration procedure not documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: web_search.rs has 1252 lines implementing WebSearchTool, DomainSearchTool, FetchPageTool with full Tool trait implementations. But grep 'WebSearchTool' in swell-tools/src/mod.rs returns no matches.

sharedStateObservations.observation: SearchRouter in search_router.rs has sophisticated query classification with configurable indicators and min_deep_length parameter. ResearcherAgent creates a simpler static classify_depth() that checks different conditions (uses 'error:' 'exception:' etc vs SearchRouter's approach). The existence of SearchRouter wasn't leveraged - worker built a parallel implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: search_router.rs::SearchRouter::classify_depth() uses quick_indicators/deep_indicators vectors with configurable thresholds. agents.rs::ResearcherAgent::classify_depth() uses hardcoded static arrays and query.len() < 50.

summary: ResearcherAgent has correct structure and passes tests, but has blocking issues: (1) SearchDepth enum is duplicated instead of reusing search_router's version, (2) new types not exported from lib.rs breaking discoverability, (3) max_iterations hardcoded violating settings convention, (4) web_search tools exist but aren't registered. The feature partially fulfills its contract but leaves architectural debt that will cause maintenance issues.
